summary: Care Attempt fallback action validated end-to-end on the live preview (mobile viewport 390x844). Button visibility, BASIC tag, 1 AP cost label, hero-switching persistence, per-hero acted-disable, AP consumption, corruption reduction (-5 on chapter 1), and acted-hero handling all behave correctly. Could not finish a winning battle with >2 Care Attempts in-session because the testing player's auto-team (Novice Guardian + Village Caretaker) has no strike-tagged skills, so a win is not reachable using Care Attempt + Stabilize alone — but param plumbing (battle.tsx passes basicAid; result.tsx parses basicAid; computeStars in clinical.ts requires lowBasicAid <= 2; CLINICAL FORM card renders 'Nx Care Attempt — efficiency star withheld' when basicAidUses > 2) is verified by code review and matches the spec exactly. No backend testing performed (frontend-only request).
action_items: Main agent should manually verify the win-with-3+-Care-Attempts result path on a hero/team that includes a strike skill (e.g. River Sludge enemy + a strike-capable hero), OR by adding an automated unit test for computeStars and the result-screen CLINICAL FORM rendering. Implementation is correct on paper but couldn't be reached organically with the default starter team during testing.

(Optional) Console warnings observed: 'shadow*' style props deprecated; 'props.pointerEvents' deprecated; 'useNativeDriver is not supported' on web. Pre-existing — not Care-Attempt-related, but worth a future cleanup.
critical_code_review_comments: battle.tsx line 195 correctly passes basicAid: String(state.basicAidUses) to /result.
result.tsx line 28 parses basicAidUses; lines 119–124 render the CLINICAL FORM card and the 'Nx Care Attempt — efficiency star withheld' line when basicAidUses > 2.
clinical.ts computeStars line 772–774 requires lowBasicAid (basicAidUses <= 2) for the 3rd star — exact match to PRD.

battle.ts applyCareAttempt enforces all guard rails (outcome, selectedHero, heroActionsUsed, AP), increments basicAidUses, decrements AP by 1, calls consumeHeroAction, and adds two log lines. Corruption reduction uses careAttemptDamage(chapter, isBoss) returning 5/4/3/2 — matches spec.
Care Attempt does NOT advance the care chain (no chainRole tracking), preserving fullChainCompleted semantics — correct.

battle.tsx render block lines 367–419 correctly inserts the Care Attempt button at the head of the selected hero's skill list with testID 'battle-care-attempt', BASIC tag, and dynamic '-N Corruption' text.

success_rate.frontend: 5 of 7 spec items fully exercised live; remaining 2 (3+ uses → 2-star + CLINICAL FORM and <=2 uses → 3-star) verified by code review only

context_for_next_testing_agent: Default onboarding via 'BEGIN CALLING QUIZ' produces a team with Novice Guardian (River) + Village Caretaker (Growth). Neither has a strike skill against air_sprite; reaching natural win requires either picking a different first enemy or seeding a stronger team. The battle screen has 4 AP per turn and 2 heroes => max 2 Care Attempts per turn. To organically reach >=3 Care Attempts AND a win, you must couple Care Attempts with skill/item kills, or use the boss/multi-system enemies whose teams include strike skills.
rca_of_issue: n/a — feature works as designed in all branches exercised.
